objective: Prevent long-context models from stalling startup by capping auto-resolved max_model_len in addition to batching safeguards.
scope: Apply minimal, reversible, fork-local runtime/config updates in src/engine_args.py, .runpod/hub.json, and aiwf.request.json only: add SAFE_MAX_MODEL_LEN_CAP for auto-resolved model lengths, retain SAFE_MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS_CAP protections, enforce chunked prefill safety for large contexts, and expose the new cap in hub advanced defaults.
acceptance_criteria: src/engine_args.py caps auto-resolved max_model_len using SAFE_MAX_MODEL_LEN_CAP (default 32768) when MAX_MODEL_LEN is not explicitly set.
src/engine_args.py caps auto-derived max_num_batched_tokens using SAFE_MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS_CAP (default 8192) so large model max lengths do not become unsafe batch-token defaults.
src/engine_args.py forces enable_chunked_prefill=True when max_model_len exceeds 32768 and chunked prefill is disabled, unless ALLOW_UNSAFE_DISABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL=true is explicitly set.
src/engine_args.py emits concise logs when runtime safety overrides are applied.
.runpod/hub.json sets ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL default to true and lowers MAX_NUM_SEQS default to 16 for safer serverless behavior.
.runpod/hub.json includes advanced SAFE_MAX_MODEL_LEN_CAP (default 32768) and SAFE_MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS_CAP (default 8192) aligned to code.
All edits remain fork-local, minimal, and reversible via documented rollback actions.
tests_expected: Run a fork-local RunPod smoke test with a large-context model (e.g., Qwen 3.5 27B) to verify startup stability and no crash from unsafe batching defaults
rollback_plan: Immediate rollback in this fork: remove SAFE_MAX_MODEL_LEN_CAP and SAFE_MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS_CAP logic plus large-context chunked-prefill enforcement from src/engine_args.py, restore .runpod/hub.json defaults (ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL=false, MAX_NUM_SEQS=256, remove SAFE_* cap entries), and revert this exact three-file change set in one commit. Operational fallback without code rollback: set MAX_MODEL_LEN explicitly and tune SAFE_* caps per endpoint. No upstream operations required.
deploy_plan: Validate in this fork only: run py_compile and JSON checks, inspect focused git diff, then run a fork-local RunPod smoke test on a long-context model to confirm the worker starts and serves without OOM/crash from auto-derived batching defaults.
threat_model: Primary risks are throughput regression from tighter defaults, behavior changes for users who previously relied on very large implicit context windows, and misconfiguration of new safety cap env vars. Mitigations: conservative defaults (SAFE_MAX_MODEL_LEN_CAP=32768, SAFE_MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS_CAP=8192), explicit escape hatch for unsafe chunked-prefill disable, concise runtime logs for overrides, and single-commit rollback across only three files.
